name: Ed Barlow
tagline: Software engineer, database architect, and published author. Building thoughtful systems for complex problems.
home_html:
Current Project: The GAME Framework for Agentic Development

I have combind my Process Based Methodology and scale-proven Technology Rules into an agentic developer Framework.

The concept is exciting. Contact me if you want to collaborate.

Key Concept: LLMs enable individual developers to create - but each creation will be structured differently and they wont work together by default

Key Concept: An Agent can conform your code projects. I use annotations and non impacting changes to expose your capabilities of your projects in a uniform way with limited/no impact to your code.

Key Concept: This same Agent can enforce best practices, branding, and security. I provide defaults which are markdown and easy to modify.

Key Concept: The Capability Catalog knows your project capabilities and advertises these as tools which YOUR agents can use (Mcp for python) - Capabilities include services, data, and servers.

Key Concept: This enables your code to talk to trusted code seamlessly - with your llm to undrestanding your enterprise capabilities as you develop

Key Concpet: and... since all your code is encapsulated, I provide a full enterprise command center... Centralized Logging/Monitorng/Alerting/Scheduling/Pipelines ...

The GAME Framework contains

  • A Control Panel provides namespaced views into your projects.
  • A Capability Catalog broadcasts your tooling in a "MCP for python" manner (transport agnostic: Rest, CLI, Async, MCP)
  • A **A Prototyper ** provides reproducable builds from base specifications - including of MY tooling - enabling customization to your needs
  • Simple Workflows enable control of your projects and change management
  • Enterprise Operations - for YOUR projects - Monitoring, Documentation, Scheduled tasks, Data Flows, and Sharing OUT OF THE BOX

Who I am

Hands On Backend Technology Leader at hedge funds, investment banks, insurance companies, and startups

Author, Speaker, Free Software Developer
